segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a festive, stylized title card featuring a decorated Christmas tree with golden ornaments and lights, displaying the text 'MIA GREEN' and a 'MAKE SURE TO LIKE AND SUBSCRIBE!' banner, flanked by two cutout images of a woman in workout attire. Light, upbeat electronic music plays in the background. The scene then transitions to a woman, identified as Mia Green, sitting in the driver’s seat of a car, wearing a light gray 'ER ACTIVE' hoodie, her long brown hair tied back in a ponytail. She speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, greeting viewers and announcing, "Hey guys, welcome back to my channel. I hope you're all doing well. Today's video is going to be a full week of workouts." An energetic electronic track with a strong beat then accompanies a montage of gym workout scenes, showing Mia performing squats with a barbell, doing leg presses, and lying on a bench with dumbbells, all set against a backdrop of gym equipment, with the text 'Full week of WORKOUTS' overlaid. The video returns to Mia in the car, where she continues her dialogue with animated hand movements and direct eye contact, explaining, "I'm showing you guys my updated workout split. I've not done one of these videos in a long time... now I'm into my bulk, I thought that you guys would want to see one. It also has been highly requested." She adds that while there haven't been "too many changes" to her workout split, it will be "mostly the same" as what she's done in the past, but she wanted to make an updated video showing her "current workouts," maintaining a conversational and energetic tone throughout her speech.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Woman in Car, a woman with long brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing a brown long-sleeve crop top and matching leggings, and over-ear headphones. She stands beside a squat rack, adjusting her grip on a barbell loaded with BLK BOX plates, preparing for a set of RDLs, stating, "and then move onto another RDL variation." She then humorously adds, "I clearly chose violence for my Monday morning." The scene transitions to a different area of the modern gym, where she is now seated on a black weight bench in front of a large BLK BOX dumbbell rack. She adjusts her over-ear headphones, announcing, "Next up guys, we have another Romanian deadlift variation." She explains her choice, noting, "Now I only usually do the one variation in my glutes workout, it's usually just barbell RDLs, but I don't know, I just had to just spice it up today, and I'm gonna do some single leg dumbbell RDLs." On-screen text appears, confirming 'Single leg dumbbell RDL's 2x8 reps each leg'. She then stands up, picks up a single dumbbell from the rack, and begins performing single-leg Romanian deadlifts with controlled form, balancing on one leg while lowering the dumbbell toward the floor and extending the opposite leg behind her. As she demonstrates, she provides detailed instructions: "get the same arm to leg, the same dumbbell in the same arm as it is the leg that's doing it," advising to "put this non-working leg to the back," and to "put my hand on my hip for some support." She continues, "bend this working leg and then come down just below knee length," explaining that the depth depends on individual height and femur length, and to "contract the glute on the working leg and come back up and keep the dumbbell super close to your leg as well." Her movements are deliberate and focused, showcasing her strength and stability in the brightly lit gym with mirrored walls and neatly organized equipment. The camera captures her from multiple angles, emphasizing her precise technique throughout the set.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a well-equipped, industrial gym with bright, even lighting, where the Woman in Car, dressed in a black hoodie and matching black sweatpants with her hair tied back, is performing barbell squats. She wears over-ear headphones as upbeat, rhythmic electronic music plays in the background. She adjusts her stance and grips the barbell, loaded with black BLK BOX weight plates, executing several controlled squats with proper form. After completing a set, she steps back, removes her headphones, and smiles briefly. Her voice is then heard explaining her routine, stating, "All right, and then I will also do just a few reps with just the barbell. No weight on it, just to kind of get a feel for the movement." She continues, "And I also like to just kind of like sit at the bottom of it like this a little just to kind of make sure my depth is as best as it could be," before remarking, "Gosh, I'm warm." An off-screen voice asks, "Is that right?" to which she replies, "You can go in front." She then announces, "Let's put 15 on," followed by, "Okay, next warm-up set," as she repositions the barbell on the squat rack. On-screen text 'Barbell squats 3x8 reps' appears, indicating her exercise parameters. The sounds of weights clanking are audible beneath the music as she prepares, later stating, "Alright, I'm gonna put another five on each side, which will make it 80 kilos," demonstrating her focused progression in strength training.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit gym, where a woman in a gray crop top, black shorts, and knee sleeves performs seated calf raises on an 'Exigo' machine with a 10-pound 'IVYBOX' weight plate. Other gym-goers, including a man in a black hoodie, move in the background amidst the black equipment and metal staircase, contributing to the active atmosphere. She continues, "I'm telling you guys, my quads workouts are more brutal than any other workout that I do. I literally leave the gym hobbling. Okay, last set," her voice reflecting both exhaustion and a touch of humor about the intensity of her training.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a dimly lit gym where the Woman in Car, wearing a dark gray sports bra, light gray leggings, black over-ear Headphones, and a RODE Microphone, performs a core workout on a Black Gym Mat. She starts with single leg raises, accompanied by heavy breathing and sounds of exertion, while upbeat, rhythmic electronic music plays in the background, and the on-screen text 'single leg raises 30 secs' is displayed. She then transitions into mountain climbers, continuing her intense effort as the text 'mountain climbers 30 secs' appears. After completing the exercises, she sits on the mat, catching her breath, and states in a slightly breathless British accent, "Okay, circuit is done. We're gonna have a one-minute rest before we do that all again." The on-screen text 'repeat the circuit three times!' is visible. The scene then cuts to the Woman in Car, now in a light gray hoodie with her hair down, sitting in the driver’s seat of a car with black leather seats. She speaks directly to the camera with an enthusiastic British accent, gesturing animatedly, saying, "All right guys, that is this week's full week of workouts all done. This video is finally ended. I'm sure this video felt like it has lasted a lifetime because it is, it's very long. I was editing it the other day and it's already at about 45 minutes and I've not included this workout yet. But even still, I hope you guys enjoyed. Please let me know if you want to see these more often, and I'd be happy to make more for you. But this is what my current split..." The car interior and suburban buildings outside are visible under natural daylight.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman, identified as the vlogger, seated inside a car, facing the camera with a focused and expressive demeanor. She wears a light gray hoodie, her long, wavy, highlighted brown hair cascading over her shoulders, and speaks in a clear, conversational, and engaging voice. She animatedly discusses her appearance, stating it won't change for the next few months, especially while she's "bulking." Using hand gestures—touching her face, clasping her hands, and moving them expressively—she expresses hope that viewers enjoyed the video, mentioning it took "so, so long to edit" due to "technical difficulties with like the mic top and stuff," noting it's a different type of video she's done, particularly "for like the whole week."
